citation: U.S. Geological Survey, 2014, Rare earth statistics, in Kelly, T.D., and Matos, G.R., comps., Historical statistics for mineral and material commodities in the United States: U.S. Geological Survey Data Series 140, accessed July 10, 2026, at https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center/historical-statistics-mineral-and-material-commodities.
comparability_warning: National REO-equivalent statistics are not partner-level HTS trade data and must not enter the DataWeb China-share, supplier-HHI, or HTS unit-value calculations.
compiled_by: 1Compiled by C.A. DiFrancesco, J.B. Hedrick, D.J. Cordier, and J. Gambogi.

notes.apparent_consumption: Estimated for specified years as production plus imports minus exports; interpolated for years listed in the embedded worksheet notes; 2000 onward calculated using estimated REO content.
notes.constant_unit_value: U.S. unit value adjusted with the Consumer Price Index to constant 1998 dollars.
notes.data_sources: U.S. Bureau of Mines and U.S. Geological Survey Minerals Yearbook and Mineral Resources of the United States.
notes.exports: Estimated rare-earth-oxide equivalent in alloys, compounds, metals, and ores exported from the United States.
notes.general: Data are calculated, estimated, or reported. See embedded notes document for more information.
notes.imports: Estimated rare-earth-oxide equivalent in alloys, compounds, metals, and ores imported into the United States.
notes.production: Contained rare-earth-oxide equivalent in U.S.-produced bastnaesite and monazite concentrates.
notes.status_codes: NA Not available. W Withheld to avoid disclosing company proprietary data.
notes.unit_value: Weighted-average U.S. unit value in current dollars per metric ton of apparent consumption.
notes.world_production: World rare-earth-oxide equivalent content of ores produced.

title: Rare Earths - Historical Statistics (Data Series 140)
unit_note: All quantities in metric tons (t) rare-earth oxide equivalent unless otherwise noted
